name: Giggle Finance
description_short: Revenue-based funding up to $15,000 (or $20,000 for repeat customers) for gig workers, 1099 contractors, and self-employed individuals — no credit score required.
description_long:
Giggle Finance is a revenue-based funding provider built specifically for the gig economy — independent contractors, freelancers, 1099 workers, side hustlers, and small business owners who are routinely underserved or rejected by traditional lenders. The company positions itself as a fast, friendly alternative to bank loans, emphasizing speed and simplicity over documentation-heavy underwriting.

Giggle Finance offers revenue-based advances up to $15,000 for new customers and up to $20,000 for returning customers. The application is designed to take approximately 8 minutes and requires connecting a bank account to verify income and revenue. Approval is based solely on demonstrated business income — not credit scores — and funds are deposited directly into the verified account as quickly as possible after approval. Repayments are structured as weekly draws tied to business revenue, meaning payment amounts flex with income.

Several features distinguish Giggle from other short-term business funders. First, the company reports on-time payments to Experian and TransUnion, giving borrowers an active path to building business credit — a genuine differentiator for gig workers who have thin credit files. Second, the revenue-proportional repayment model reduces the risk of fixed payments overwhelming cash flow during slow periods. Third, Giggle offers a prepayment discount for borrowers who want to pay off their balance early, which is not universal in the merchant cash advance space.

Giggle Finance is a legitimate option for the narrow segment it serves — independent contractors and small operators who need fast liquidity and cannot qualify elsewhere. However, the website does not disclose factor rates, annual percentage rates, or total cost of capital, which is a standard concern with revenue-based financing. Prospective borrowers should request a full fee schedule and repayment breakdown before accepting funds, as revenue-based advances can carry effective costs significantly higher than traditional small business loans.
pros: No credit score check — approval based entirely on business revenue
8-minute application process with no lengthy paperwork
Funds deposited quickly after approval — designed for same-day access
Reports on-time payments to Experian and TransUnion, building business credit
Repeat customers can access up to $20,000
Prepayment discount available for early payoff
Weekly repayments tied to revenue — scale down during slow periods
cons: Factor rates and total cost of capital are not disclosed on the website
Maximum $15,000 for new customers may be insufficient for larger capital needs
Requires ongoing bank account connection for revenue verification and repayment
Only available to businesses with proven, demonstrable revenue — brand-new ventures likely won't qualify
Revenue-based structure is not a traditional loan and may not suit borrowers needing long-term fixed-rate financing
best_for: 1099 workers and independent contractors needing fast working capital
Self-employed individuals with steady revenue but limited credit history
Small business owners needing under $15,000 quickly without paperwork
Repeat borrowers seeking higher limits up to $20,000
diagnosis: Giggle Finance is best suited for gig economy workers, 1099 contractors, and self-employed individuals who need fast working capital under $15,000 and have been turned away by traditional lenders due to credit or documentation barriers. The main caveat is that factor rates and true cost of capital are not disclosed upfront — borrowers should request a full repayment breakdown before accepting, as revenue-based advances often carry effective APRs well above conventional small business loans.
services: Revenue-based business funding up to $15,000 (new customers)
Revenue-based business funding up to $20,000 (repeat customers)
Bank account verification for income assessment
Business credit reporting to Experian and TransUnion
Funding for independent contractors and 1099 workers
Funding for side hustles and freelancers
